attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who commits to long-range moves and preserves finishing speed for small group sprints at the finale.
team_tactics: Minimal team support visible; operates as autonomous breakaway specialist hunting stage wins from reduced groups.
terrain_preference: Stages conducive to breakaways that fragment into small finishing groups where tactical sprint skills determine outcomes; less suited to pure climbing or mass sprints.
vulnerability: Late-race chase groups with strong pursuers create pressure; managed only 10-second margin against determined chasers at Giro 2022.
key_patterns: Commits to breakaways with intent to finish, not just animate race.
Retains sprint speed in small groups after extended breakaway efforts.
Effective at age 35+ in selective breakaway scenarios requiring experience and timing.
form_trajectory: positive: first Grand Tour stage win in 3 years indicates resurgence in breakaway finishing ability at 35+
